summary: Cathy O'Neil appeared on NPR's 'To The Point' to discuss predictive policing and whether Big Data can prevent crime. She notes she was the most skeptical among four guests, which included a journalist, a techno-futurist, and a public safety director. The brief post serves primarily to share the podcast link with her readers.
key_points: O'Neil participated in an NPR discussion on predictive policing
She positioned herself as the most skeptical voice among the panel guests
The panel represented diverse perspectives: journalism, tech optimism, and law enforcement
money_quotes: I was definitely the most skeptical of the four guests
key_insight: O'Neil signals her characteristic skepticism toward Big Data claims in policing, though this post is primarily a podcast announcement rather than substantive analysis.
